article_title: VoltaGrid Raises $1 Billion From Blackstone and Halliburton at More Than $10 Billion Valuation, Acquires Propell to Bring AI-Data-Center Power in House
summary: All five snapshots reachable; three executive quotes verbatim; financial specifics traced. Allowlist gaps and 1-char title overflow are non-reader-facing.

content_preview.title: VoltaGrid Raises $1 Billion From Blackstone and Halliburton at More Than $10 Billion Valuation, Acquires Propell to Bring AI-Data-Center Power in House
content_preview.summary: Houston gas-microgrid maker VoltaGrid took $775M in primary and $225M in secondary from Blackstone Tactical Opportunities and Halliburton, and is buying its main supplier Propell to lock in a 7.5 GW order book through 2030.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

VoltaGrid, a Houston-based maker of gas-powered microgrids that supply behind-the-meter power to AI data centers, announced on May 11 a $1.0 billion strategic equity investment from Blackstone Tactical Opportunities and Halliburton, alongside a definitive agreement to acquire its primary manufacturing partner Propell Energy Technology, according to the company's [Blackstone-issued press release](https://www.blackstone.com/news/press/voltagrid-announces-1-billion-strategic-equity-inv...

recommendations: Add blackstone.com, news.bloomberglaw.com, energynow.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — Blackstone is a canonical primary source for its own portfolio announcements; Bloomberg Law and EnergyNow are routine financial/energy trade press.

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear News piece, 589 words, covers the equity investment / Propell acquisition / financial context cleanly. Includes appropriate 'What We Don't Know' on undisclosed Propell purchase price and named customers.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all five reachable snapshots. source-0 (Blackstone press release) and source-1 (GlobeNewswire distribution) confirm verbatim all three executive quotes: Nathan Ough 'This partnership with Blackstone is a powerful endorsement of the platform we have built and the role VoltaGrid is playing'; William Nicholson 'a highly differentiated platform addressing one of the most important infrastructure needs of the AI era: reliable power'; Jeff Miller 'reflects our shared focus on long-term solutions for the world\'s most demanding power environments'. Both also confirm $1.0 billion / $775M primary / $225M secondary split, 7.5 GW order book, Propell 1978 founding / ~1,000 employees / Granbury TX, QPac platform. source-2 (Bloomberg Law) confirms '>$10 billion' valuation. source-3 (EnergyNow) confirms the 'materially reduce execution risk across VoltaGrid's 7.5 gigawatt order book between now and 2030' verbatim and the Fitch Ratings EBITDA-to-$1.1B-by-2028 / 90% Blackstone share / pre-deal sale-vs-IPO process. source-4 (TechStartups) is a light roundup; the article uses it only for general AI-infra thesis context.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every specific verified: $1.0B / $775M / $225M, 7.5 GW order book, >$10B valuation, ~300 MW/month production target, Propell founded 1978 / ~1,000 employees / Granbury TX, projected $1.1B EBITDA by 2028 (Fitch), 90/10 Blackstone/Halliburton split, prior sale-or-IPO process.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, factual, appropriately attributes opinion-tinged framings to their sources.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of VoltaGrid.
concerns: Title is 151 characters — 1 over recommended max of 150. Cosmetic, non-blocking.
recommendations: Future submissions: trim titles to <=150 chars.
Expand source allowlist for canonical primary issuers (Blackstone, BloombergLaw, EnergyNow).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean APPROVE. Three executive quotes verbatim, every financial specific traces to a snapshot, every claim correctly attributed. Title-length warning is cosmetic.
